mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE increases are driven by seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show positive directionality during industrial production contractions, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% during periods of high volatility.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, higher borrowing costs constrain corporate leverage and consumer spending, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and falling total nonfarm payrolls confirms a structural labor market deterioration that depresses long-term earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction between UNRATE and PAYEMS yields a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows, or 60-day forward returns remain positive when both labor indicators deteriorate simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real GDP growth directly boost corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as markets price in stronger economic activity.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below 50% across multiple rolling test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening manufacturing output that improves corporate earnings outlooks, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns turn negative during industrial production expansions, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% when inflation exceeds 3% annually.
